description: PR-10 mock-exam assembler + readiness engine review (companion to pr10-mock-assembler.md).
method: operator adversarial self-audit of mock_exam.py + readiness_report (determinism, holdout isolation, allocation correctness, readiness math) plus a 14-test suite, THEN an independent agent code review dispatched before commit per governance §16.0. The independent review returned two findings; both are recorded and resolved here.

operator_findings.problem: time_management_performance reported 0.0 when no timing data existed, unfairly penalizing readiness
operator_findings.fix: exclude the component (None) when time_management.total is 0
operator_findings.note: independent reviewer inspected the committed code and confirmed the fix is correct (F1).

independent_findings.problem: no-timing-data must not read as a 0.0 penalty
independent_findings.resolution: the reviewer confirmed the committed code already excludes the component when time_management.total is 0; no further change required.

independent_findings.problem: a holdout scenario assembled into a mock (and thus revealed by mock_learner_view) but left UNSUBMITTED hit `if item is None or iid not in submissions: continue` before the burn append, so it was never added to burned_holdout_ids. An exposed-but-unanswered scenario could therefore be re-drawn in a later mock and re-counted as an unseen 'fresh' scenario.
independent_findings.resolution: burn is now computed from EXPOSURE, not submission. New helper `exposed_holdout_ids(mock)` returns every assembled holdout id; grade_mock sets burned_holdout_ids = exposed_holdout_ids(mock) as the single source of truth, independent of what was submitted. Fresh-scenario accuracy still counts only SUBMITTED holdout scenarios. Regression tests added: test_unsubmitted_holdout_scenario_is_still_burned, test_burn_set_equals_exposure_even_on_partial_submission, test_exposed_holdout_ids_are_all_assembled_holdout.

verified_invariants: determinism (sha256 rank, sorted domains, allocation sums to count)
scenarios drawn holdout-only; practice scenarios never used as mock scenarios
grade_mock returns ids/accuracy only, no holdout stems/choices
burn: seen holdout ids excluded, and now burned = every EXPOSED holdout id (F2 fix)
readiness weights renormalized over available components; div-by-zero guarded; hard blockers fire
note: The independent review reported AFTER the operator's self-review but BEFORE this PR-10 follow-up commit. F1 was already resolved; F2 (major) is resolved in the follow-up. Per the PR-9 lesson, no clean-review claim was made until the independent review actually reported.
